title: Allowing downgrades to a clear-text protocol is security-sensitive
description: Allowing downgrades to a clear-text protocol is security-sensitiveAnalyze your coderesponsibility-trustworthysecuritySecurity HotspotcweThe usage of HTTPS is not enforced here. As it is possible for the HTTP client to follow redirects, such redirects might lead to websites using HTTP.As HTTP is a clear-text protocol, it is considered insecure. Due to its lack of encryption, attackers that are able to sniff traffic from the network can read, modify, or corrupt the transported content.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Using curl without --proto "=https" flag is security-sensitive. The HTTP client may follow redirects to clear-text HTTP protocol, which is insecure and can be sniffed, modified or corrupted by attackers.
